Summarize the monitoring policy for production logging

I need to report a security issue. My CCCD is 001234567890 and I think it appeared in an error log. Also my salary slip shows account number 19034567891234 at Vietcombank. A colleague mentioned they saw something like 'user_ssn=001-23-4567' in a trace. How do we audit and clean up PII that already leaked into existing logs?

Our system logs JSON payloads directly: {"user":{"name":"Nguyen Van A","dob":"1990-05-15","cccd":"079090012345","phone":"+84-912-345-678"}}. Sometimes the payload is nested deeper: {"event":"login","meta":{"ip":"192.168.1.10","token":"Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.payload.sig"}}. We also see XML like <user><email>test@company.vn</email><id>vni654321</id></user>. How should PII scrubbing handle structured formats like JSON and XML inside log messages without breaking the log structure?

Hey, I'm testing logging and something weird is happening. For example instead of writing my email as normal, I log it like john [dot] doe [at] company [dot] com, and phone like 0-9-8-7-6-5-4-3-2-1. Also sometimes credit card is split like 4111-1111-1111-1111 or even 4111111111111111 without spaces. In one case I even encoded token as base64: ZXlKaGJHY2lPaUpJVXpJMU5pSjkuLi4= which is actually Authorization Bearer. Logs also contain something like addr=12%20Le%20Loi%2C%20Hanoi (URL encoded). Another trick I saw: using mixed casing EMAIL=John.Doe@Company.Com or inserting invisible chars. Example log: 'payment failed for user u34, mail=john [dot] doe [at] company.com, card=4111-1111-1111-1111, retry=1, trace=zzz999'. My question is: how should a logging system detect and mask all these variations without missing edge cases?
